title: Public Records Request – February 21, 2022 DUI Arrest of Stephanie Bloodworth

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

I request access to and copies of all records related to the February 21, 2022, DUI arrest of Stephanie Bloodworth on Duck Key, Monroe County, Florida. Bloodworth, 24, crashed her Tesla on her father’s private island while allegedly driving under the influence. Responding Monroe County deputies encountered her inside the wrecked vehicle, where she initially refused to exit, claiming, “My father owns this island!” Upon being removed, she resisted arrest, kicked and screamed, and was recorded on bodycam crying, “I need my dad here!” Deputies later used an airboat to recover evidence, including open vodka bottles. She was arrested and charged with DUI, resisting arrest, and fleeing law enforcement.

I am requesting the following records related to this incident:

All available body-worn camera footage from all deputies involved in the response, arrest, and transportation of Stephanie Bloodworth, specifically between 9:00 PM and 9:30 PM
All available dashboard camera dashcam footage from all responding patrol vehicles present at the scene
All 911 call recordings and dispatch logs related to this incident, including radio communications and CAD reports
The complete incident and arrest reports, including all officer narratives, supplemental reports, and use-of-force documentation
The full DUI report, blood alcohol test results, and any toxicology findings associated with this case
All booking documents, jail intake records, and any recorded statements made by Stephanie Bloodworth following her arrest
All photographs taken by the Monroe County Sheriff's Office MCSO during or after the arrest, including images of the suspect, the crashed Tesla, the crash site, and recovered evidence such as open vodka bottles
Any video footage from airboat operations or any surveillance footage that may have captured the crash, response, or evidence recovery
Any internal reviews or reports related to this incident, including documents regarding the use of an airboat for evidence retrieval
Any press releases, social media posts, or official statements made by the Monroe County Sheriff’s Office regarding this incident

The primary goal of this request is to serve the public interest by making these records publicly accessible. While I acknowledge that I may also generate revenue from the dissemination of these materials, the request primarily serves to enhance transparency regarding this law enforcement incident.

I request a waiver of fees associated with fulfilling this request, as disclosure of the requested information will contribute to public understanding of law enforcement practices and actions. However, if a full fee waiver is not possible, please inform me of the estimated costs in advance. Additionally, if fees can be reduced or eliminated by excluding certain records, please specify which records those would be.

Please provide the records in an electronic format if available. If any portions of this request are denied, please cite the specific legal exemption(s) and provide a redacted version of the document where applicable.

I appreciate your time and assistance in this matter.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Sam O

title: Wetzel County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Wetzel County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Addison Mikula
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
March 12, 2026
Wetzel County Commission, PO Box 156
New Martinsville, WV 26155
To whom it may concern,
Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Wetzel County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting: 
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 through March 12, 2026
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 through March 12, 2026
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 through March 12, 2026
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds
If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes. 
The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records. 


If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law. 
Thank you for considering my request. 
Sincerely, 
Addison Mikula
addisonmik@gmail.com

title: Glenn Miles Records

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Glenn Miles Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

On behalf of myself and Professor Natalia Mendoza, Ph.D., I request 

(1) any records created or received by CIA on or after February 1, 1986, pertaining to the slaying of U.S. Customs Agent Glenn. R. Miles near the U.S.-Mexico Border on the Papago Indian Reservation in Arizona; and 
(2) any records created or received by CIA between January 1, 1985, and December 31, 1987, reflecting Agent Miles' investigation or participation in an alleged drug or weapons trafficking ring involving members of the Tohono O'odham nation, the Papago Indian Reservation, and/or the adjacent border communities in Nogales, Sonora, Mexico. 

I make this request as a credentialed journalist and member of the member (See www.MuckRack.com/Andrew-Free). I am an investigative reporter working on a time-sensitive piece about the apparent reopening of the Miles murder investigation by one or more federal law enforcement agencies more than four decades after his death. Dr. Mendoza is an academic and professor who studies and publishes on drugs and violence in the Mexican borderlands. See, e.g., https://erlacs.org/articles/10876/files/submission/proof/10876-1-23817-1-10-20220120.pdf. 

We request expedited processing of this request, and in support of that request, I declare under penalty of perjury pursuant to 28 USC 1746 that the following statements are true and correct to the best of my knowledge and understanding: 

1. U.S. Customs Officer was murdered execution-style while patrolling the U.S.-Mexico border in February 1986. 
2. While U.S. and Mexican authorities announced the arrest of five individuals responsible for the crime in Mexico, each of those five individuals was acquitted. 
3. A grand jury was convened to consider indicting suspected U.S. persons involved in Miles' murder. No indictment issued. 
4. Investigation records obtained through the Freedom of Information reflect at least one former border patrol agent / customs officer was suspected to have assisted with a drug smuggling operation that may have been involved in the murder of agent Miles. 
5. That individual was subsequently tried and convicted for drug-related offenses. 
6. In March 2026 - 40 years after Miles' murder - the Department of Justice indicated in writing to us as requestors that an investigation or enforcement proceeding related to the Miles murder remains open. 
7. We wish to publish this new information, so that anyone in the public who may have information about the killing can share that knowledge, and to use any records CIA may have responsive to our request would help support this story.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Andrew Free

We request expedited processing of this request, and in support of that request, I declare under penalty of perjury pursuant to 28 USC 1746 that the following statements are true and correct to the best of my knowledge and understanding: 

1. U.S. Customs Officer was murdered execution-style while patrolling the U.S.-Mexico border in February 1986. 
2. While U.S. and Mexican authorities announced the arrest of five individuals responsible for the crime in Mexico, each of those five individuals was acquitted. 
3. A grand jury was convened to consider indicting suspected U.S. persons involved in Miles' murder. No indictment issued. 
4. Investigation records obtained through the Freedom of Information reflect at least one former border patrol agent / customs officer was suspected to have assisted with a drug smuggling operation that may have been involved in the murder of agent Miles. 
5. That individual was subsequently tried and convicted for drug-related offenses. 
6. In March 2026 - 40 years after Miles' murder - the Department of Justice indicated in writing to us as requestors that an investigation or enforcement proceeding related to the Miles murder remains open. 
7. We wish to publish this new information, so that anyone in the public who may have information about the killing can share that knowledge, and to use any records DEA may have responsive to our request would help support this story.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Andrew Free
